steps.title: Compare the two poems
steps.content: The passage gives Alexander's poem as an example of ekphrasis because it meditates on a painting. Eady's poem is introduced as another poem that takes a painting as its subject.

steps.title: Use a similarity transition
steps.content: The matching transition means in the same way, which fits two parallel examples of ekphrastic poems. Choice A and Choice B signal contrast, and Choice D signals cause and effect. The answer is C.
